project: SWE Mentor App - AI Mentor LangChain Backend
description: Build the LangChain agent backend: install deps, create tools, 3-layer prompt system with 4 coaching modes, middleware stack, and SSE streaming API route. This powers the AI mentor chat feature.

userStories.title: Install LangChain dependencies and create project structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need LangChain packages installed and a config module so the agent can be initialized.
acceptanceCriteria: Install in apps/web/: langchain @langchain/core @langchain/anthropic @langchain/langgraph @langchain/textsplitters
Create apps/web/src/lib/langchain/config.ts with ChatAnthropic initialization (claude-sonnet-4-5, temperature 0.7, maxTokens 2000)
Export createMentorAgent() factory that accepts tools array and middleware array, wires model + MemorySaver checkpointer
Use createReactAgent from @langchain/langgraph/prebuilt for the agent
Create apps/web/src/lib/langchain/index.ts barrel export
Add ANTHROPIC_API_KEY to apps/web/.env.example (do NOT create .env with real key)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Follow existing patterns in apps/web/src/lib/ (see supabase client setup). The web app uses Next.js 16 with App Router. Use MemorySaver for dev — document PostgresSaver path for prod in a code comment. Reference docs/ai-mentor-langchain-reference.md section 'Agent Factory Pattern' for createReactAgent usage.

userStories.title: Create mentor tools (get_career_goals, get_progress_summary, get_recent_checkins, search_user_context stub, get_career_ladder)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need LangChain tools that query Supabase so the agent can access user data during conversations.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/lib/langchain/tools.ts
get_career_goals tool: reads profile (role, target_role, focus_areas, company_name) for the user from config.configurable.userId
get_progress_summary tool: reads user_streaks + daily_metrics aggregate (last 7 days) for the user
get_recent_checkins tool: reads last N check-ins with energy, wins, blockers (accepts count param, default 7)
search_user_context tool: STUB that returns 'RAG not yet configured — will be wired in a future update'
get_career_ladder tool: queries career_matrices by user's company_name (fuzzy ILIKE match), falls back to generic template if no match
All tools use Zod schemas for input validation via @langchain/core/tools DynamicStructuredTool
All tools use the Supabase server client from apps/web/src/lib/supabase/server.ts
All tools return JSON.stringify(data) for model consumption
Export tools as an array
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: CRITICAL AUTH PATTERN: Do NOT use createClient from apps/web/src/lib/supabase/server.ts — that uses cookies() from next/headers which won't work inside LangChain tools. Instead, each tool receives userId via config.configurable.userId AND a pre-authenticated Supabase client via config.configurable.supabaseClient. The API route (US-005) creates this client from the Bearer JWT token using createClient(url, anonKey, { global: { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${jwt}` } } }) and passes it down. DRY: create a shared getSupabaseClient(config) helper in apps/web/src/lib/langchain/utils.ts that extracts the client from config.configurable and throws if missing. All 5 tools use this helper — NEVER construct their own client. The check_ins table has columns: id, user_id, type (morning/evening), energy_level, focus_area, daily_goal, quick_win, blocker, reflection, created_at. user_streaks has: user_id, current_streak, longest_streak, last_check_in_date. career_matrices has: id, company_name, source. level_definitions has: matrix_id, level_code, level_name, technical_expectations, leadership_expectations, etc.

userStories.title: Create 3-layer prompt system and 4 coaching modes
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a sophisticated prompt system that provides personalized coaching based on the user's career level and emotional state.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/lib/langchain/prompts.ts

BASE_COACHING_PROMPT constant with Layer 1 principles: diagnose before advising, be tactical not theoretical, teach the why, always give a concrete next step, reference their actual data, make output screenshottable
LEVEL_PLAYBOOKS map keyed by role transition strings (e.g. 'intern->software_engineer_1') covering: intern->SE1, SE1->SE2, SE2->senior, senior->staff, staff->principal
TACTICAL_GUIDES object with templates for: PR descriptions, blocker escalation, impact communication, design docs, brag doc entries
COACHING_MODES map with 4 modes: Supportive (energy <= 2), Socratic (high energy + has direction), Direct Consultant (has blockers), Exploratory (open-ended/curious)
buildSystemPrompt(profile, mode) function that assembles all 3 layers + selected mode into a single string, injecting user-specific variables (name, role, target_role, company, focus_areas)
detectCoachingMode(lastMessage, energyLevel) function that analyzes message content + energy to select the appropriate mode
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: See docs/ai-mentor-langchain-reference.md sections '3-Layer Prompt System' and '4 Adaptive Coaching Modes' for detailed content. The prompt should use {{placeholder}} style variables that buildSystemPrompt replaces. Profile type comes from packages/shared/src/database.types.ts (Tables<'profiles'>). The intern playbook should focus on: learning velocity, asking good questions, seeking feedback, return offer positioning. Energy level comes from the user's most recent check-in. DRY: create a replacePlaceholders(template, vars) utility used across all prompt assembly.

userStories.title: Create middleware stack (summarization, dynamic prompt, rate limit, cost limits, error handling)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need middleware that manages conversation summarization, dynamic prompts, rate limiting, and error handling.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/lib/langchain/middleware.ts
Summarization middleware config: uses claude-haiku-4-5 model, triggered when messages exceed context threshold, keeps last 10 messages
Dynamic system prompt middleware: fetches user profile from Supabase, detects coaching mode, calls buildSystemPrompt() to set system message before each model call
Rate limit middleware (beforeModel hook): calls check_and_increment_chat_usage RPC, throws RateLimitError if not allowed
Tool error handling middleware (wrapToolCall hook): catches tool failures, returns graceful error ToolMessage instead of crashing
Model call limit: max 10 model calls per invocation
Tool call limit: max 15 tool calls per invocation
Export assembled middleware array in correct order: summarization -> dynamic prompt -> rate limit -> cost limits -> error handling
Create RateLimitError class that includes remaining count and reset time
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: MIDDLEWARE PATTERN: @langchain/langgraph createReactAgent does NOT have a built-in 'middleware array' parameter. Implement middleware as wrapper functions around agent invocation in the API route, or use createReactAgent's messageModifier for system prompt injection. The middleware functions should be composable: each returns a function that wraps the next. CRITICAL AUTH: The rate limit middleware must call check_and_increment_chat_usage RPC using the JWT-bearing Supabase client (passed via config.configurable.supabaseClient from the API route), NOT a service role client. The RPC has an auth.uid() guard that will THROW if auth.uid() is NULL (which happens with service role). The RPC returns a TABLE — access as data[0].allowed, data[0].current_count, data[0].remaining (it's an array, not a single object). DRY: the RateLimitError class should be in apps/web/src/lib/langchain/errors.ts, reused by both middleware and API route. Include remaining count and resetAt (compute as midnight UTC of current day + 1 day).

userStories.title: Create API route with SSE streaming
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a Next.js API route that handles chat requests and streams responses as Server-Sent Events.
acceptanceCriteria: Create apps/web/src/app/api/mentor/chat/route.ts
POST handler: receives { message, conversationId } from request body
Auth validation: extract Bearer token from Authorization header, verify with Supabase auth.getUser(token)
If no conversationId, generate UUID and create conversation row in Supabase
Save user message to messages table before invoking agent
Invoke agent with agent.stream() using streamMode 'messages', thread_id = conversationId, configurable: { userId }
Stream response as SSE: Content-Type text/event-stream, Cache-Control no-cache, Connection keep-alive
Each token sent as: data: {"token":"...","conversationId":"..."} followed by newlines
Send data: [DONE] on completion
After stream completes, save full assistant response to messages table
GET handler at same route: returns { remaining, limit, resetAt } for authenticated user by querying daily_metrics
Error handling: 401 for auth errors, 429 for rate limit (with remaining/resetAt in body), 500 for other errors
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a Next.js App Router route handler (not pages API). Use NextRequest/NextResponse. CRITICAL AUTH PATTERN: The validateAuth(request) helper in apps/web/src/lib/langchain/auth.ts must: (1) extract Bearer token from Authorization header, (2) verify with Supabase auth.getUser(token), (3) return BOTH the user object AND a JWT-initialized Supabase client: createClient(url, anonKey, { global: { headers: { Authorization: `Bearer ${token}` } } }). This client is passed to agent via config.configurable.supabaseClient. Do NOT use the cookie-based server.ts client. SSE STREAMING: The agent.stream() with streamMode 'messages' yields [message, metadata] tuples. IMPORTANT: check `message instanceof AIMessageChunk && typeof message.content === 'string'` before emitting — tool-use messages have content as an array, not a string. Skip non-string content blocks. RPC ARRAY: When querying daily_metrics for the GET handler, the RPC returns an array — use data[0]. Compute resetAt as midnight UTC of current day + 1.
